campaignEntries.rawText:
Benedicta, fondly known as Bennett by her parents, is the only child of the ^"De Lentis". Her parents were the founding leaders of an uprising against @"Corwin Thalorian"’s unjust rule. 
After years of fighting, they found they were pregnant and soon after made the difficult decision to abandon the fight. This sparked outrage and controversy within the movement. They called themselves the ^"Ember Alliance".
They fled in the dead of night. However, their movements were betrayed to @"Corwin Thalorian" and they were ambushed. Some of her parents paid with their lives to ensure that the others and Bennett would escape and live. This act of martyrdom ultimately sparked more rebellions against the ^"Thalorian Dynasty", even though her parents' own rebellion was quashed.
Bennett was born on the ship her surviving parents used to escape. As she grew, it slowly became apparent that Bennett was blessed by the act of her parent’s sacrifice, or at least that is what her living parents told her. Her eyes shone like a cat’s in the dark, and her blood had a pearlescent sheen to it.
Bennett’s childhood was spent on many ships her parents worked on, and they continued to run from the reach of the ^"Thalorian Dynasty". She was shaped by her parent’s heroism, sacrifice and grief. In her teenage years, the remains of the family settled in #"Zhelesyr", opening their own smithy to support themselves.
Now grown, she carries the legacy of a revolution she never chose—haunted by the lives lost, driven to make sense of their sacrifice and find her own purpose. She decided it was time for her to see the world and gain experience as an individual away from her protective parents.

Full Name: p'Neep-rüdab
Age: Full Grown (whatever the equivalent of a 30 human years is in Bullywug years)
Connections:

@"Bauble"

Personality:
Observant, calculating, a bit impulsive
Cheeky & sarcastic when he does talk. A little silly.
Got a bit of a sailor's mouth iykwim
Strong hyperfixation on projects - will prioritize these over everything else when it comes down to the wire
Loves to solve a problem.
Loves to drink.
In this pirate business because he's anti-imperialist and wants to fuck shit up. Also, the deaths of his best friends and loves of his life haunts him while he's on land. He's constantly looking for them and becomes restless when they're docked.

Background:
Basing !"Bullywug" society loosely off [this](https://forgottenrealms.fandom.com/wiki/Bullywug#google_vignette) forgotten realms wiki page. I'm imagining his tribe to be "slightly" less brutal but still carrying the big thematic elements.

Was a quiet but mischievous youth, claiming he was going to conquer the untamed wilds with his inventions and getting beat up for it. (But also getting revenge on the bullys with traps and stuff)

Learned how to use a bunch of artisan's tools on his own and managed to impress his tribe with new gizmos and killing tools so he was largely left alone even though he didn't have the same physical prowess as the other males in his tribe.

Somehow he pisses the tribe leader off, he has to fight and then he loses his tongue and gets kicked out and left for dead. His best friend @"B'Rieph" & other best friend/crush @"Bauble" decide to run away together. They successfully escape but are then caught up in an imperialist raid(?) of a village they'd settled into. Bishop presumes @"Bauble" & @"B'Rieph" are dead as he spends 3 years in forced servitude on an imperial ship (or something similar. I want him to have a healthy dose of anger towards the establishment so as to fuel the fire of being a pirate) before his ship is attacked by pirates and he's picked up to be a deckhand on the pirate ship.

When we start the campaign it's been a total of 8 years since he was separated from @"Bauble"& @"B'Rieph".

Character Growth/Story Points:

SURPRISE! His best friends are not dead and have in fact been mostly okay.

DOUBLE SURPRISE! @"B'Rieph" and @"Bauble" managed to escape with most of @"Bauble"'s clutch of eggs and are now raising 3 baby !"Bullywug"s together. They deeply mourn the death of Bishop and that he'll never get to see his kids grow up.

Completely unsuited to conveying complex ideas, but well-suited to agreeing on a price, asking where the bathroom is, and telling someone to turn right.

Mechanics: Attempts to convey (persuasion, intimidation, deception) or understand (insight) complex or philosophical ideas in common have disadvantage.

"This is a nice sword so you should pay me more for it." is fine.
"Charity is essential. Perhaps the most important similarity when it comes to religion and giving is that all faith leaders stress the importance of practicing. The more we give, the easier the act of giving becomes. The idea of viewing giving as a muscle came up repeatedly across sects. By making giving part of our routines, we can grow the giving muscle. While different religions suggest or require different percentages or offerings, faith leaders agree that it is fine to start small. In fact, people who tell themselves that they have to wait to give until they can larger sums unfortunately never start to give. Just like you build muscle with a few reps and lighter weights to start, giving just a few gold or a small percentage is an important part of growing your capacity for giving. With that in mind, are you sure you can't sponsor our entry into the city?" has disadvantage.

The Order of Bone, often simply referred to as "The Order" in #"Arctoriam" due to its ubiquity across national and cultural lines, is a widespread Magic hunting organization dedicated to the end of all magic in the world. 

Magic is a disease, and the Order is the Cure. 

The Order of Bone is not directly tied to any government, though many governments fund local chapters of the order.

Externally the Order is referred to as a single monolithic entity but internally, the Order is comprised of several sub-orders:
^"Order Of Skull-Gleaming"
^"Order Of Orbital-Unblinking"
^"Order Of Marrow-Bleeding"

1.  **Lyra** (Wife): Elara was a skilled herbalist, known for her gentle wisdom. She sadly succumbed to an illness despite all efforts to treat her, leaving a significant void in the family.
    
2.  **Garen** (Oldest Son): Garen followed in his father's footsteps as a ranger but met his end during a skirmish with marauding bandits while protecting their village.
    
3.  **Lirael** (First Daughter): Lirael was an adventurous spirit who sought treasures beyond their land's borders and tragically fell into the enchantment of an ancient curse while exploring distant ruins.
    
4.  **Oswin** (Second Son): Oswin was renowned for his storytelling and bravery but was unfortunately caught in the crossfire of two warring factions he tried to negotiate peace between.
    
5.  **Mira** (Third Daughter): Mira had inherited her mother’s knowledge of herbs but perished unexpectedly during childbirth, unable to be saved even by fellow healers' best efforts.
    
6.  **Fennor** (Third Son): A talented bard whose songs are still sung across taverns today; Fennor's life ended when he heroically jumped into floodwaters to save stranded villagers during a ferocious storm.

@"Rene" first encountered the ^"Order of Bone" when he was a kid. Back then, he worked as a courier of sorts. His job was to train messenger birds that delivered correspondence from his home city, a service the local ^"Order Of Bone" chapter often co-opted for its own use. Thanks to a casual disregard for confidentiality, he grew up aspiring to what he read of them – the travels, danger, coin, and purpose.

@"Rene" joined the ^"Order Of Bone" as a teenager. He was trained to fight by his girlfriend and future wife, @Evaine. While @"Rene" initially believed their work was a necessary evil serving the greater good, his own resolve proved more susceptible to guilt with time.
    
At some point over his decade of service, @"Rene"stopped killing the people he was sent to hunt and started helping them instead. In his travels, he met a ship captain from the new world who was willing to help smuggle magic users to relative safety. Rayner would find them, hide them, arrange their escape, then inform the Order of their death. He got away with it for longer than expected until, inevitably, the lies caught up to him.
    
@Evaine discovered his betrayal first. She followed him on his next hunt and confronted him at port on the smuggler’s ship. When he admitted to going behind her back for years, she set fire to the ship and tried to kill everyone on board, especially him. He was outmatched and forced to run. Hoping to evade her pursuit on the open ocean, he got aboard the next ship he saw going anywhere else as fast as possible.

campaignEntries.rawText:
Addressed to @"Bennett"

Dearest @"Bennett",

@"Bennett", I’m so sorry to read that you were forced into a position like that. I know that you have lived with the burden of literacy your whole life, and your new friends are only recently literate, if at all. I know you, B, and if you’d had any other choice at all, I know you would have taken it. 

  

B, I know you trust your friends, but it seems more and more that you need to keep yourself protected from their carelessness. Things like this increased crew count, it doesn’t seem like they understand the way you do what it’s like to live a life of literacy surrounded by the illiterate. I certainly don’t know the sea the way you do, B, so maybe I’m off-base. I agree, finding a literate crew that large seems like an impossibility. 

  

If you do decide to strike out on your own, I know you’ll be alright. With the way you inspire loyalty I’m certain a good portion of the literate crew would choose to accompany you. If you could make your way here I’m certain I could help you acquire a small ship of your own to set out with. 

  

In the meantime, you’d of course be welcome to stay here and relax for a time in Vaelcor’s reach. For that matter, if you just need a few weeks off the sea and away from work, I’d welcome the visit. Our shipmaster has been given your ship’s name and will ensure that you have a berth here should you ever make your way north. I’d be overjoyed at the chance to see you again, and to explain to your friends the errors in their thinking. 

  

While you’re here I’m afraid I can’t allow you to steal me away to the sea. I shouldn’t like to see the bounty my parents would put on your head if such a thing were to be the case, and I’m afraid that my pampered self is likely unsuited to the rigors of such a life. Now, if you’d ever like to leave the life of hardship behind and become a full-time courtier, please, do let me know. 

  

For that matter, if you receive this letter when it first arrives, the Silver Tern will be resupplying before coming back to us, and Captain Vayne would be happy to see you to our shores. Even if you don’t return with him, @"Bennett", I’d recommend speaking with him. He’s an excellent sailor and swordsman and I’m sure he’d be happy to exchange a few tricks with you. 

  

I hope your tasks in Vallume went according to your hopes, and that any legal jeopardy your crew was in evaporated as a lot of mistaken identity. I know you wouldn’t be caught involved with anything untoward. I look forward to hearing all about what you learned in Vallume, and how you learned it, when we see one another again in person. 

  

As to me, I’ve just rejected the hand of yet another eligible bachelor after two months of courting, and the two debutantes after him after only a few days each. He was nice enough, but after the initial phase of getting to know one another I found we had nothing sufficiently in common to keep talking about. The two women were worse, with no seeming interests of their own beyond the latest fashion at court. You know me, B, I certainly don’t shy from making a fashion statement myself, but how my parents could present to me these suitors with no other interests is beyond me. 

  

My parents, in what they describe as necessary and I decry as an attempt to punish me, are throwing a ball in a month or so to try to trap me into a new relationship. The ball I’ll enjoy. The attempts of the various parties to see me locked into a relationship much less so.

  

Be well, B. And please write back soon. 

  

Yours,

@"Bastian Malivorna"

Thramnium is an extremely strong and resilient metal by weight, with the added benefit of a cumulative limited magical corruption resistance (it is able to absorb a certain amount of magical corruption during its lifetime with no ill effect). 

Thaumium's creation is a closely guarded secret. It is fabricated only by the ^"Order Of Bone", and within the Order only the ^"Order Of Marrow-Bleeding" are taught the process of crafting it. 

A common assumption among others in the order, based on the name, though not confirmed, is that it is somehow crafted of the bones of dead -"Manawracked".

%6089-02-03 to %6091-05-07

A period of time defining a gradual change in the way magic operates in the world. 

Prior to this period, magic was extremely difficult and required tremendous resources to accomplish even trivial effects. Years of study and preparation and thousands of platinum of gold would go into a spell to enchant a sword with a sharper edge. Magic was weak, but it was safe. It accomplished what it set out to predictably and formulaically. 

By the end of this period, magic had become significantly easier to cast, to the point where anyone could manage to learn it with some effort. Spells could be cast by those who knew how with only a token effort. Magic had become strong, but dangerous. Spellcasters and those around them started to become Manawracked. 

The power structures that had existed prior to this period broke down, as in the middle magic had reached a perfect balance of safety and usability to allow those who embraced it to affect enormous change in the world at their will. 

By the end of this period, the world had changed, not only in structure but also in culture. Where before magic-users were revered as devoted scholars or the most pure of belief, after they were seen as dangers to their community and hunted. 

While theories abound regarding exactly what caused the chance in magic, none of them have ever been proven. People have adapted to live in the new world that the change in magic left behind. Some have made peace with it, while others word to discover why and reverse the change. Others still work to push the change further and give magic even more power.

Achim Gabriels — _The Iron Whisper._  Myrenthal Military
A gaunt man who speaks softly so every deckhand must lean in; his calm breaks tempers and mutinies alike. Commands a fleet of slow barges that always arrive first.
Katla Dodge — _The Gilded Gale._  
A gambler’s grin and a duelist’s reflex; she wagers her course on the turn of a coin enchanted to always choose chaos. Her crew never know where they’ll land, but never regret it.

Gudrun Molnar — _The Lantern Bearer._  
Keeps his lanterns burning always, for the dead he cannot leave behind. Sailors follow his light through fogs others deem cursed.

**4\. Captain Caven "Crowfeather" Druel** — _The Sky Corsair._  
Once a sky‑pirate, now hunts his former kin across the trade winds. The black feathers in his coat are said to be trophies from lightning strikes he survived.

**5\. Captain Sera Quin** — _Mistwarden of the East Shoals._  
Her eyes are clouded white, yet she sees better through fog than any pilot alive. Guides convoys by sound—her humming keeps the spirits of drowned sailors at bay.

**6\. Captain Dareth Solen** — _Breaker of Chains._  
A freed galley slave who bought his own ship with prize money and pride. He never keeps bonds aboard—every sailor’s a partner, not property.

**7\. Captain Relka Dorn** — _The Pearl‑Eater._  
Loves storms and silence, hates gods and nobles. Claims she swallowed a sea‑spirit’s heart; her laughter rolls like thunder.

**8\. ** — _The Glass Knight._  
Wears mirrored plate even in the tropics. Uses illusions so the enemy never knows where his blade—or ship—truly is.

**9\. Captain Jeska Virel** — _The Ember’s Wing._  
Bard turned privateer; sails a fire‑rigged frigate powered by song. When her crew chants in rhythm, the ship burns brighter but never ashens.

**10\. Captain Torven Malcray** — _The Deep Broker._  
Trades in things best left unspoken—weather rights, ghost maps, and promises not yet made. His ledger’s said to weigh more than his conscience.

During ~"Watch" the future ^"Captains" we realize that they’re being spied on by @"Zhessir Kraakel". @"Zhessir Kraakel" flees when addressed.
@"Zhessir Kraakel" returns at sunrise with the rest of the ^"Castaways of the Unknown Dinosaur Island". The party convince them it’s ok to bring @Caspian back to the #"Unknown Dinosaur Island Treetop Encampment" under the condition that he is tied up.
At the #"Unknown Dinosaur Island Treetop Encampment", the party learns the ship anchored in the north is a scientific expedition from #"Calvatharn". The party is healed and stocked on basic supplies while they plan to steal the ship by liberating enough !Calvatharn slaves to fight back against their masters. The promise to return to inform the ^"Castaways of the Unknown Dinosaur Island" if their plan to steal the !Calvatharn ship solidifies.
The party advances further into the jungle only to get lost for a while. They run into an &"Allosaurus" that is easily taken down. 
They come to a point where they hear chatter in ~"Calvatharn" up ahead, but nobody can understand it besides @Bishop. They follow the noise to a regiment of !Calvatharn soldiers and follow them to #"Calvatharn Dig Site 1187", where they find scientists are trying to obtain some kind of eggs without the parent dinosaurs noticing. The party plans to single out a soldier when they’re away from the rest of their company and convince them to defect. They will return to their regiment and spread word. Then we can attack the commander without interference from the slave-soldiers. The plan is effective in taking out the first commander, and the party gains 10 soldiers on our side.
@Pot returns to the #"Unknown Dinosaur Island Treetop Encampment" to inform the ^"Castaways of the Unknown Dinosaur Island" that the plan is moving forward.
The rest of the party returns to #"Calvatharn Dig Site 1187" to take out the rest of the commanders at the outpost. They end up with about 30 slave-soldiers and 5 miners on their side, whith the knowledge that about 80-90 soldiers remain at the main camp with their commanders. They plan to incite more rebellion among the main camp and steal the ship next time.

all and sun‑burned, with weather‑creased features and sea‑green eyes that never seem to rest; a constant glint of mirth at the edge of fatigue. His coat’s a faded indigo lined with silver thread, stitched and re‑stitched a dozen times. A single bronze feather pin holds his hair back—a keepsake from the first gryphon that raced his ship and lost.

Idealist and obsessive in equal measure. Alaric believes there is _always_ something beyond the next storm—a new island, a lost route, a forgotten civilization—and he’ll wager everything on that faith. To crew he’s father, friend, and tyrant all at once: fair in punishment, reckless in command, generous in rum.

Bards claim he once sailed straight through an aether‑tempest to rescue a scholar trapped in the cyclone’s eye. Scholars claim he delivered manuscripts thought drowned for centuries. Pirates claim he talks to the wind itself; that it _answers_.

Keeps a faded brass sextant with an empty gem‑socket. Says the missing gem fell from the sky, and until he finds where it landed, his voyage isn’t over.

1. Lady Varissa "The Whispercloak" Darnel
Race: Half-Elf
Specialty: Espionage and intelligence
Personality: Smooth, unflappable, and almost preternaturally calm. She prefers secrets as her currency and rarely raises her voice — her whispers carry power enough.
Signature Style: Operates from behind veils and intermediaries. No one’s quite sure what she truly looks like.
Rumor: Claims she knows the real name of every noble in the capital — and uses that knowledge to hold their pursestrings.
2. "Iron-Tooth" Garric Thane
Race: Dwarf
Specialty: Smuggling, illegal trade, and artifact recovery
Personality: Gruff but pragmatic. He runs his crew like a business syndicate, not a rabble.
Mannerisms: Constantly chews something — sometimes tobacco, sometimes gemstones.
Rumor: His real teeth were crushed during an interrogation years ago; he replaced them with enchanted iron fangs that can bite through locks.
3. Silk-Finger Nali
Race: Human
Specialty: Pickpocketing, infiltration, urban performance
Personality: Flashy, charming, and always loves an audience. If she weren’t a thief, she’d be a stage star.
Appearance: Dresses in fine silks that billow dramatically, despite their impracticality.
Rumor: Keeps a secret ledger of every noble she’s ever robbed — and how much they paid her to keep it quiet afterward.
4. The Jackal Prince
Race: Unknown (possibly shapeshifter)
Specialty: Assassinations and disappearances
Personality: Cold, calculating, believes in balance — the coin for blood must be equal to the weight of the soul taken.
Symbol: A jackal’s pawprint scorched into parchment left at the scene.
Rumor: Only ever works contracts delivered before dawn, and none who have seen his true form live long enough to describe it.
5. Old Mother Quent
Race: Gnome
Specialty: Poison crafting and information brokerage
Personality: Sweet as honey on the outside, venomous beneath. Speaks in grandmotherly endearments while dosing opponents with truth serums.
Quirk: Keeps a collection of "pets" — venomous insects in little glass bottles.
Rumor: The entire thieves’ guild network owes her at least one favor, whether they know it or not.

In @"Caspian"'s teen years his antics would frequently involve run-ins with the authorities. At the age of 16, he was given the option of either serving at a deck-swab aboard a navy vessel or prison time. @Caspian has been sailing with the *"Zns Thalassir" for 6 years.
logEntries.title: Caspian - Teen Years

logEntries.rawText:
@"Caspian" Grew up with a drunk absentee father, running about a shanty town attached to #"Arinthal Harbor". 

Half-raised by #"Seasong Haven" where he would stay at when he didn’t feel like sleeping in a drafty cottage. The mistress, @"Gloriana Maris" would try to keep him and make sure he attended lessons but @Caspian was never one for sitting still or book learning.

When he wasn’t at school, he was often found listening to tales of the sea from @"Elias Braith" at one of the more rundown taverns. He became enamoured with stories of places beyond the map’s edge and beings never met before. Most people treated @"Elias Braith" as a drunkard and a liar, but @Caspian swore to follow his example in the future.
logEntries.title: Caspian - Early Years

logEntries.rawText:
@"Isabel Coronado Du Zof" has ordered the *"Zns Thalassir" to run through ship combat maneuvers despite the rain that morning. The crew continues to drill at her command, under threat of beatings by @"Sloane" and his compatriots until they are exhausted from running to-and-fro about the ship. Hands of open-blisters from clutching the ropes. At least twice a sailor has been flung overboard from the sharp change of direction and the crew had to haul them aboard and start over.

At some point, @"Isabel Coronado Du Zof" is informed of a distress flare spotted on the horizon. Thinking only of the publicity this will garner, she commands @"Sloane" to give chase immediately.
logEntries.title: Caspian - The Incident

logEntries.rawText:
@"Bastian Malivorna" wrote a letter to @"Bennett"
I’m ecstatic to hear that the life of a sailor has been both lucrative and exciting. The tales you’ve sent of your adventures certainly don’t do them justice and I await the chance to interrogate you about them in more detail in person. 
Of course, I could learn more about your journeys if you had included the name of your newest ship in your correspondence. 
Invited her to visit if she came to #"Durathia".
Cautioned her about trusting people that fear her.
As to the other person, and what they say about your condition… I don’t know what to say. I’ve still not come across anything in my studies, but I keep looking. I’m glad you found something. What can I say about your renewed interest in literature? It’s never been a subject I’ve been willing to pursue, but I also hold no connection to it like you do. It’s supposed to be incredibly dangerous to the reader, but you’re one of the most capable people I know. I don’t know, B. Just be safe. That’s all I can ask. I’d never tell you not to try to help innocents, but take care of yourself, even if it’s selfish of me to ask. 
I have learned so much since we last spoke. My father has hired a new bevy of tutors for my recent interests. In accordance with what you mentioned in your letter I will be adding the New World’s common tongue and hand signs to my studies as soon as a suitable tutor can be found. My studies with our blademaster continue to underwhelm him of my progress, but my social tutors continue to trumpet my limited successes in that field as making me a very eligible bachelor indeed. I’d rather keep studying. Languages and music are still my favorites, and I’ve learned some truly beautiful arias recently. I’ve started trying to write some of my own, but my success has been limited. 
I feel odd writing to you about my life, as boring as it sounds compared to yours. I wish sometimes that I could  just leave this all behind and join you on the sea, but I know I’m not cut out for it as romantic as it is to read about in books. 
The family social events continue, though at this point my lack of interest in whatever bachelor or debutant is brought my way is becoming a thorn in my mother’s side. And to be honest, B, I’m starting to fear that I won’t get to choose my match in the long run if I don’t soon. I don’t want to end up in a loveless marriage like my parents. It scares me, and I don’t really know what to do.
